item.title: Berkshire cash pile climbs to $380bn as stock sales streak hits 14th quarter
item.description: Greg Abel to address shareholders at first gathering since he succeeded Warren Buffett

item.title: Canada’s diamond industry begins a long goodbye
item.description: The closure of Rio Tinto’s Diavik mine is the beginning of the end for a lifeline of the remote Northwest Territories

item.title: Adidas sprints ahead of Nike in recovery race
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: LIV’s dim prospects and the NBA’s proposed ‘tanking’ fix

item.title: The physical world strikes back
item.description: The Iran war is a reminder that geographic facts rather than digital tech shape our lives

item.title: The problem with healthy life expectancy
item.description: A crude blend of very different statistics is not the best tool for the job

item.title: Meta stock might look cheap if it weren’t for Mark Zuckerberg
item.description: Not only is the Facebook boss spinning a lot of plates, but his worldview is not like that of a typical chief executive

item.title: Wall Street traders post triple gains of European rivals
item.description: Europe’s biggest investment banks missed out on gains from commodities in quarter marked by oil swings

item.title: Dividend payments from UK companies up ‘over a fifth’ on last year
item.description: Total payouts were £16.4bn during the first quarter of 2026

item.title: The luxuries I can’t give up (even as prices rise)
item.description: I know it’s ridiculous to pay £32 for shower gel, but not all luxuries are frivolous

item.title: Trump’s Iran war withers Kenya’s roses and strands its tea
item.description: Conflict has crushed Gulf markets and pushed up air freight and shipping costs

item.title: South Africa now has a power surplus, says Eskom chief
item.description: Long a byword for failure through years of power shortages, the state utility is now grappling with how to raise demand

item.title: English councils to trial Google AI tool to speed up planning decisions
item.description: Artificial intelligence will make recommendations on whether to grant or refuse projects

item.title: Paranoid parenting in the age of AI
item.description: It’s an illusion to think we can robot-proof our kids’ education choices

item.title: Libya reaps oil bonanza from Iran war price surge
item.description: Crude output has risen to highest level since 2013 as demand soars to replace lost barrels from the Gulf

item.title: ‘I don’t see it as fighting’: the nun challenging Citibank and Palantir
item.description: Sister Susan Francois believes shareholder advocacy holds the key to ‘better’ corporate behaviour

item.title: It’s over. Net-a-Porter has sent me packing
item.description: The fashion etailer is banning customers who return too many items. But who is to blame for the rise of the serial-returner?

item.title: Q&A: Am I a professional investor?
item.description: The City regulator is set to change the rules on client categorisation

item.title: Wealthy raid their pensions to avoid UK inheritance tax shake-up, advisers warn
item.description: Unused retirement savings could be taxed as part of overall estates after next year’s reforms

item.title: ‘I’m fighting two wars. One against the Russians. And one inside myself.’
item.description: A drone pilot and her all-women unit are waging a new kind of warfare on Ukraine’s eastern front

item.title: The fight over data centres at the heart of Japanese cities
item.description: Japan is getting ready for a huge surge in AI facilities — and complaints from nearby residents

item.title: How safe is your money from cyber attack?
item.description: Claude’s Mythos AI model raises the stakes as it finds vulnerabilities in financial software

item.title: Is China decoupling on food?
item.description: If this is pursued with the same energy as industrial policy, it will upend the agricultural economy

item.title: US warns Europe of delays to arms shipments as Iran war drains stockpiles
item.description: Deferred shipments could include those used in Ukraine’s defence against Russia

item.title: Trump’s Iran war leaves US with sharpest fuel shock in G7
item.description: Petrol and diesel prices are rising more swiftly in America than other major economies including the UK and Canada

item.title: Apple credits ‘most popular’ ever iPhone for booming sales
item.description: John Ternus pledges to continue ‘deliberateness and discipline’ in first comments since being named next CEO

item.title: Spirit Airlines prepares to cease operations as bailout talks founder
item.description: Donald Trump says US still ‘looking’ at a rescue for bankrupt budget carrier, but would only proceed ‘if it’s a good deal’

item.title: Trump says he will raise tariff on EU vehicles to 25%
item.description: Levies on cars and trucks exported to US will be imposed ‘next week’ unless companies manufacture at American plants

item.title: Jane Street employees paid $9.4bn in 2025
item.description: Trading firm brought in record $40bn amid financial market volatility

item.title: New Nasdaq rules offer SpaceX free liquidity
item.description: Indexing methodology will provide a comfortable cushion for pre-IPO shareholders

item.title: Private credit group Ares draws nearly $20bn from investors
item.description: Real estate and infrastructure commitments help offset weakness in group’s core business
